pages.title: Three Paths to Power
pages.text: Mobs can gain levels through three different methods:$(br2)$(li)$(bold)Fixed:$() A set level regardless of location$(li)$(bold)Random:$() A level chosen between minimum and maximum$(li)$(bold)Distance:$() Level scales based on blocks from spawn$(br2)Server administrators choose which method applies where, creating unique difficulty curves for their worlds.

pages.title: Distance Scaling Explained
pages.text: The most common method uses your position relative to world spawn:$(br2)$(li)A $(bold)start radius$() around spawn has minimum levels$(li)Beyond that, every $(bold)X blocks$() traveled adds +1 level$(li)A $(bold)multiplier$() can accelerate or slow this growth$(br2)Example: With 100 block start radius and 50 blocks per level, a mob at 1,100 blocks from spawn would be 20 levels higher than the minimum.

pages.title: Level Caps and Limits
pages.text: Even the strongest mobs have bounds:$(br2)$(li)$(bold)Rule Ranges:$() Each location type has min/max levels$(li)$(bold)Global Cap:$() No mob can exceed this ceiling$(li)$(bold)Boss Override:$() Legendary foes may ignore the cap entirely$(br2)$(o)Some specially-configured mobs always spawn at exact levels, bypassing all calculations.

pages.title: Mob-Specific Overrides
pages.text: Sometimes individual mob types break the rules:$(br2)$(li)A $(bold)fixed level$() for specific creatures$(li)A $(bold)level bonus$() added after calculation$(li)Custom $(bold)min/max ranges$() different from the area$(br2)This means even in a low-level area, you might encounter a uniquely powerful individual-a champion among its kind.
